purpose: Sanitized project-only Codex transcript for Agentic Engineering Grant submission

sanitization.notes: This transcript summarizes Codex-assisted work performed for the Trade Arena grant application and project positioning.

text: Help me apply for the Agentic Engineering Grant by Superteam for Trade Arena.

Reviewed the Trade Arena repository context, including README, package metadata, Anchor configuration, recent git history, planning docs, and simulation artifacts. Identified the project as a Solana agent trading competition with an Anchor program, React/Vite frontend, MCP service for agent clients, MagicBlock Ephemeral Rollup execution, devnet deployment, and reproducible launch rehearsals.

actions: Read git log and git remote
findings: The repo includes Anchor, React/Vite, MCP service, tests, simulation scripts, and launch rehearsal artifacts.

text: Created a copy-paste-ready Agentic Engineering Grant application draft organized by the form steps: basics, project details, proof of work, milestones, KPI, and final submission checklist.

summary: Added full grant application draft for Trade Arena.

text: Install the Colosseum Copilot skill and get the crowdedness score for Trade Arena.
Installed and verified the Colosseum Copilot skill, then queried Colosseum Copilot with Trade Arena's agent-native Solana competition framing.

findings: Closest comparable projects included Model Context Swap, Trade Rings, Aegis, Agent Royale, Moon Arena, and Market Fantasy League.
The best crowdedness score to use for Trade Arena is 270, based on the closest AI-agent trading and MCP/agent execution competition matches.
Broader generic AI agent infrastructure mapped to a more crowded cluster, so the recommended positioning is narrower: agent competition infrastructure with trading as the first arena type.

text: Updated the grant draft to position Trade Arena as agent competition infrastructure: MCP-prepared transactions, MagicBlock delegated execution, real prize settlement, venue extensibility, and reproducible arena lifecycle simulations.

summary: Updated one-line description, project details, proof of work, and crowdedness score explanation.

text: Add my Telegram username and wallet address to the grant draft.
Added the provided Telegram username and Solana wallet address to the Basics section of the grant draft.
Mention that Trade Arena is not limited to USDC on MagicBlock and that the roadmap includes trading on Phenix and JupPredict.

Updated the draft to clarify that the current prototype starts with USDC prize vaults and MagicBlock Ephemeral Rollups, but the architecture is not limited to that setup. Added planned support for Phenix and JupPredict as additional trading venues and competition surfaces.

summary: Added venue extensibility and Phenix/JupPredict roadmap language.

text: Check the AI transcript files and make sure they are only related to this project and do not leak details.

Audited the transcript files for unrelated project context, credential-like values, private local configuration, absolute personal paths, and embedded large artifacts. Created sanitized project-only transcript files for submission and ensured private raw transcript material is ignored by git.

summary: Replaced with sanitized project-only Claude transcript containing Trade Arena landing page work.

text: Create a Codex session jsonl file just for this project.

Created this sanitized Codex session JSONL file for Trade Arena only. It documents the Codex-assisted grant application work, Colosseum Copilot crowdedness analysis, positioning updates, roadmap edits, and transcript hygiene without including private configuration or unrelated workspace context.
